channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ASX declines on coal and gold miner weakness amid global market volatility
item.description: Australian shares experienced a notable decline on Tuesday, driven by weak earnings from Samsung and heightened geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. The S&P/ASX 200 Index dropped 27.1 points...

item.title: Chiz Escudero's Role in Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte has entered its second phase, with Senator Chiz Escudero serving as the presiding officer despite his controversial past. Escudero, who previously ...

item.title: PS Plus July 2026 Game Lineup: Confirmed Titles and Titles Leaving the Service
item.description: The PlayStation Plus service for July 2026 continues to offer a mixed selection of games across its Essential, Extra, and Premium tiers. While the quality of titles varies, the month may see a shift i...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Faces Sexual Assault Allegations and Political Pressure
item.description: Graham Platner, a 41-year-old oysterman and progressive Democrat running for the U.S. Senate in Maine, is facing mounting pressure to withdraw from his campaign following new sexual assault allegation...

item.title: AI Enhances Journalism, Not Replaces It—Experts Warn on Ethical Use
item.description: The article discusses how artificial intelligence (AI) should complement, not replace, quality journalism. Experts at the Pan-Atlantic University Media Roundtable 2026 emphasize that AI must support j...

item.title: U.S. Proposes Overhaul of Nuclear Radiation Safety Regulations
item.description: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is proposing significant changes to radiation safety protocols for nuclear power plants, sparking debate among experts and industry stakeholders. The propo...

item.title: Sarah Palin supports Ron Duguay through his stage IV colon cancer fight
item.description: Former Alaska governor Sarah Palin has been a steadfast supporter of her partner, Ron Duguay, since he was diagnosed with stage IV colon cancer in late 2024. The couple, who have been in a long-distan...

item.title: Tragic Shooting in Compton Claims Lives of Aspiring Nurse and Community Activist
item.description: Two separate shootings occurred during Fourth of July block parties in Compton, California, killing three people and injuring four. Eric Washington, a former government aide and community organizer, a...

item.title: Ombudsman Dismisses Mao Aplasca Over Senate Shooting Incident
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman has ordered the dismissal of retired police major general Mao Aplasca for his role in the May 13 shooting incident at the Senate premises. Aplasca, former acting sergeant-a...

item.title: India's E20 Petrol Rollout: Impact on Vehicles and Future Ethanol Blends
item.description: India has completed its nationwide shift to E20 petrol, which contains 20% ethanol, ahead of the 2030 target. While the government claims E20 is safe for existing vehicles, concerns persist about redu...

item.title: Renata Ford, Wife of Rob Ford, Dies After Legal Battle Over Estate
item.description: Renata Ford, the wife of former Toronto mayor Rob Ford and sister-in-law to Premier Doug Ford, has died, according to a statement from Premier Doug Ford. The news was confirmed by the premier, who exp...

item.title: Terrorist Attack Claims Nine Lives at Ziarat Police Post
item.description: A terrorist attack on a police post in Balochistan’s Ziarat district resulted in the martyrdom of nine police personnel, including two senior officers, according to officials. The incident occurred in...

item.title: Kahama Authorities Demolish Controversial Wall After Community Opposition
item.description: Authorities in Kahama, Shinyanga Region, demolished a wall that blocked public access routes after strong community opposition. The structure had sparked debates over land use and traditional pathways...

item.title: Choosing a Caesarean in the Context of Maternity Service Concerns
item.description: Sharon Gaffka, a reality TV star and political activist, discusses her decision to opt for an elective caesarean section amid concerns over the state of maternity services in England. The article high...

item.title: IEC tightens digital campaign regulations to combat election disinformation
item.description: The article discusses the Electoral Commission of South Africa's (IEC) initiative to strengthen regulations governing digital election campaigns to counter the threat of disinformation, particularly f...

item.title: Samsung Reports Record Operating Profit Amid AI Demand Surge
item.description: Samsung Electronics, the world's largest memory chipmaker, has forecast a massive 19-fold increase in second-quarter operating profit compared to the same period last year, driven by sustained demand ...

item.title: Labor’s Housing Tax Reforms: A Step Toward Affordable Housing Solutions
item.description: The Albanese government has passed significant reforms to capital gains tax and negative gearing, marking a long-overdue shift in Australian housing policy. These changes aim to address decades of poo...

item.title: University of Nairobi Promotes Prof. Faith Apolot Okalebo to Full Professor
item.description: Prof. Faith Apolot Okalebo, a renowned pharmacologist at the University of Nairobi's Faculty of Health Sciences, has been promoted to Full Professor after years of dedicated research, teaching, and me...

item.title: Matlala's Legal Team to Testify at Madlanga Commission Amid Corruption Probe
item.description: Lawyers representing Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala, an alleged underworld figure in custody on charges of attempted murder, fraud, and corruption, are set to appear before the Madlanga Commission. The commis...

item.title: Portable Defends Remi Tinubu Over Small Business Advice Controversy
item.description: The article discusses how Nigerian singer Portable defended First Lady Remi Tinubu after her advice to unemployed women to start small businesses like selling akara and roasted corn sparked controvers...
